body:
We have to make each day left in this campaign count.
#
[](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93171?refcode=em20250801-9211&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9211%2E614464%2EdKz113)
Peter, we have fewer than 100 days to go until Election Day!
I'm the only Democrat in the country who can flip a Governor's seat blue, and we're in the homestretch now. **[Before I lay out all we're doing to win the biggest race of 2025, will you consider splitting a contribution between my campaign and State Democrats Committee to power our work?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/93171?refcode=em20250801-9211&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9211%2E614464%2EdKz113)**

---
Since launching this campaign, I have visited communities all across the Commonwealth — from Farmville to Fairfax, Richmond to Roanoke — to hear directly from Virginians about the challenges they face.
_And that's informed what we communicate and how we spend our resources._
My opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, has shown she's in lockstep with Trump's agenda. She downplayed the White House laying off thousands of Virginia workers by saying, "don't fret." And she boasted that Trump's disastrous tax law does "so many great things." _We're talking about a policy that cuts $1 trillion from Medicaid and slashes resources for public schools here._
I've given voters my word: **As Governor, I will always put them first and stand up to the chaos coming out of Washington.**
We can't be defined by what we're against — we must focus on moving our Commonwealth forward.
That's why I have announced my plans to grow our workforce and raise the minimum wage, to reduce the cost of childcare, and to make life more affordable from healthcare and prescription drugs to housing costs and utility bills.
With the days left in this campaign, I'm counting on a surge of grassroots donations to:
✅ Visit every corner of Virginia.
✅ Reach undecided voters with ads and counter the GOP's attacks.
✅ Build the largest organizing operation our Commonwealth's ever seen.
It's a major undertaking, but I know this team is not intimidated by hard work. No matter what the National Republicans throw at us, we'll be ready to respond.
**[Everything depends on us raising what we need from grassroots donors. clean_body: We have to make each day left in this campaign count. Peter, we have fewer than 100 days to go until Election Day! I'm the only Democrat in the country who can flip a Governor's seat blue, and we're in the homestretch now. If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: Since launching this campaign, I have visited communities all across the Commonwealth — from Farmville to Fairfax, Richmond to Roanoke — to hear directly from Virginians about the challenges they face. And that's informed what we communicate and how we spend our resources. My opponent, Winsome Earle-Sears, has shown she's in lockstep with Trump's agenda. She downplayed the White House laying off thousands of Virginia workers by saying, "don't fret." And she boasted that Trump's disastrous tax law does "so many great things." We're talking about a policy that cuts $1 trillion from Medicaid and slashes resources for public schools here. I've given voters my word: As Governor, I will always put them first and stand up to the chaos coming out of Washington. We can't be defined by what we're against — we must focus on moving our Commonwealth forward. That's why I have announced my plans to grow our workforce and raise the minimum wage, to reduce the cost of childcare, and to make life more affordable from healthcare and prescription drugs to housing costs and utility bills. With the days left in this campaign, I'm counting on a surge of grassroots donations to: ✅ Visit every corner of Virginia. ✅ Reach undecided voters with ads and counter the GOP's attacks. ✅ Build the largest organizing operation our Commonwealth's ever seen. It's a major undertaking, but I know this team is not intimidated by hard work. No matter what the National Republicans throw at us, we'll be ready to respond. If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: I'm grateful to have you on my team, Abigail Spanberger Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats.com). Not authorized by any federal candidate or candidate committee.

Read more of your neighbors’ Solidarity Notes today, and share online with your friends and followers to bring these messages of hope to their feed: In unity, Bruna and the UWD team | Follow us on: | | | | |

name: With Honor Action
subject: Bridging the TRICARE Gap: With Honor Action Backs Bipartisan Health Care Fix for Military Families 🏥
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY WITH HONOR ACTION, INC
body:
| | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/1906af32b9c3/bats-brews-and-a-birthday-salute-to-the-us-army-14053588?e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| | | |
---
| | | | [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=6b654cfc7e&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| Friends,

Did you know that, unlike civilian health care insurance plans, military dependents under TRICARE can only remain on their parents' policy until they are 21 or 23 if they are full-time students?

That is why With Honor Action supports the bipartisan [Health Care Fairness for Military Families Act](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=1f0a491547&e=33f53a5b1a), introduced by a bipartisan pair of For Country Caucus members: Rep. Pat Ryan, a former Army intelligence officer deployed to Iraq, and Rep. Jen Kiggans, a former Navy helicopter pilot who served in the Persian Gulf. Right now, military dependents lose TRICARE coverage at 21 or 23, forcing families to pay thousands in extra premiums. This bill would fix that, letting young adult dependents stay covered until 26, just like civilian families.

Drawing on their own military service, Reps. Ryan and Kiggans are leading a common‐sense effort to alleviate a significant burden on the families who serve our nation.

🔗 You can read more about the bill [here](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=6173adcb7a&e=33f53a5b1a).
---
|
---
|
# **With Honor CEO Rye Barcott: Our mission is to help fight polarization by helping to elect and support veterans who [work] across party lines.**
---
| [ ](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=737c464653&e=33f53a5b1a)
---
| With Honor Action is leading the charge to bridge America’s political divides by supporting veterans in Congress who take a pledge to work across party lines. As With Honor Co-Founder & CEO Rye Barcott shared on 13th & Park, today only 20% of Congress has military experience—down from 70% decades ago—and the incentives to work across the aisle are scarce. Yet, through With Honor’s work, the bipartisan For Country Caucus has grown to 37 members and is tackling critical issues like national security, national service, and veterans' affairs. Rye emphasized that veterans and their families remain one of America’s strongest bridges across political lines, and With Honor is committed to harnessing that trust to get things done.

In a time of deep division, these are tangible ways veterans are uniting the country, moving meaningful legislation forward, and modeling the mission-first mindset our nation urgently needs.

🔗 You can watch the full interview [here](https://withhonor.us16.list-manage.com/track/click?u=80940e6d1b1e08928d721cf8d&id=e328955c38&e=33f53a5b1a).

body:

---

Peter,

An American Airlines plane's landing gear caught fire on the Denver runway. Passengers had to evacuate down slides while smoke poured out the side of the jet.

**And they call me _crazy_ for not flying.**

I’ve said it before, and I’ll repeat it:

_“I don’t like the sea. I don’t like the skies. I don’t like deep caves. I don’t want to go to space.”_

I’m not paranoid. I’m grounded. Literally.

**This isn’t about fear, it’s about freedom of thought.**

We’ve been trained to ignore our instincts, trust institutions that don’t earn it, and hand over control for the sake of “convenience.” But I’d rather take the long road and see America for what it is, not 35,000 feet above it, and not through a screen fed by the corporate state.

We used to value simplicity, be grateful, and enjoy life without needing to post it from Mykonos or Mars.

**[I’m running for U.S. Senate in Minnesota in 2026 because it’s time to remember normal.](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=63ce427bd4574794970b355b55b803eb&_e=MDAukdUJhUur8S50iwCDPeVuy7XYg-KR5P-pKTrRAd714tez0aIjitkMEd7w7GpwfQP6TCbp4TwR8749B66A_lUjsbOFgj9k8za75cpm2DL1bsgu0FBEh14YHFoSpnTMAJtfeiWLLnknw8Vu4bHeU6PEnnsUtGlbUUbfMtI7WwgPkym9DeBX8hhFOsIrtRVIsSXaRu9lNPMFmsnjRkN4YtcR-an6ey4HbbCojdnP4kimtOEzLFtuzD2Ld7ZGzDs8aG9z1zbUI_59NRDKoYOdXjSI5sG_uCMPZRK-zXiFTH9IOyFhtlnbhbnCS42trPSICvHRoFPwSau8n_U0J83ZwVKR5FJP46s1AJSocQNvh2wO4LGlvUShZj1_M5SVjhxgiIGwRFYTb-dFIl01gOAmOHtDxJF27gE7Poysi8LQHU0g7z0zpixZBQaiDgBBufMX-gC7V0i5beMcQzBuUcQ93IEjhS8z2ldAPif5EATwDV4Xj85Rki0A1LGGdTiGgv_PlvEJ9p-b71iI-mRimFkzFQgcbUW1YRstJCp77kFxhGbxSluuoZmOSkgxt6mqAzmP)**

If you’re tired of the lies, the fear-mongering, the phony progress, then stand with me.

| [ **Support the Movement** ](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=63ce427bd4574794970b355b55b803eb&_e=MDAukdUJhUur8S50iwCDPeVuy7XYg-KR5P-pKTrRAd714tez0aIjitkMEd7w7GpwfQP6TCbp4TwR8749B66A_lUjsbOFgj9k8za75cpm2DL1bsgu0FBEh14YHFoSpnTMAJtfeiWLLnknw8Vu4bHeU1p6yDvvmRaY1H2SYyeUbNgUD5BOaVhndC9aMwjDEKkgxIy2Y637RnNix--ctJBUatNW8LX3Kb8J7fYrqqBl07lsiaFImmrbx5HKmklmTT9UOgWSF-VNcxaS0rZI_gbKv-jEWeVnMOIe4gSYerEca28iZ0Y-s0Gy4rI671XhsNBPmLErAyGrRN2RiALze6VHzO_hMx8h21XQ8SH5XznpKrf5TyrszIDeIzsiy1y3hq79cqw6TlC3-oyUJdmQYXhK8qc17oSC67-D0AvfxEEzIF0S7mAWVAnmYeUb8CSqgDL48pOryyb8IY3w6TiuCwzssw5OEGfu2SAwzoX6PeMh9q8zdJrqd_tJ9mGBoHQ1y7ady5Ojr-NJVN93iyRu0PRge3BMqETxAgZFkQV156_IYQTCk32SH1JPsm6ihhqZvWW1)

[ **Chip in $5 to help me fight** ](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=63ce427bd4574794970b355b55b803eb&_e=MDAukdUJhUur8S50iwCDPeVuy7XYg-KR5P-pKTrRAd714tez0aIjitkMEd7w7GpwfQP6TCbp4TwR8749B66A_lUjsbOFgj9k8za75cpm2DL1bsgu0FBEh14YHFoSpnTMAJtfeiWLLnknw8Vu4bHeU1p6yDvvmRaY1H2SYyeUbNgUD5BOaVhndC9aMwjDEKkgtkNgW0JPNzh8MMVGYFk22mJ5rpX_TdJIRrurj08sPucUbtAx-2zQjQOq2U0phDCDXKFVmVnXTEkhYBG5MKKens-4ENW6bn8dibqQmNJsGVZk28t760VImcmd1kHIR3_fx6XPkWLD6nfdOJ6GHNIzeDyj-QHTv0mzFJ0jXNyKhpqatvMsGaz-LN2O5io3ChNqLh-wxfbJqiiN-GK-avIbhTsMPyx2OAbtAt9l6Xizm1-NT1GrMYwZPqhD5LU_ig0oSCIdKv5XEdfOmfk9P41wJVeY9lkof6w6OJIlir1kIrzIZ1PuN500y3rnAxm6BLQ2fDrnR0Ht2r1mwKN-OYS8v5i_2kOqQ2FBHXDxXxI4juJrrS_tKGvPqu8UnUFfWICa)
---

Let’s bring back common sense. Let’s bring back normal.

_Royce White_

[](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=63ce427bd4574794970b355b55b803eb&_e=MDAukdUJhUur8S50iwCDPeVuy7XYg-KR5P-pKTrRAd714tez0aIjitkMEd7w7GpwfQP6TCbp4TwR8749B66A_lUjsbOFgj9k8za75cpm2DL1bsgu0FBEh14YHFoSpnTMAJtfeiWLLnknw8Vu4bHeU6PEnnsUtGlbUUbfMtI7WwgPkym9DeBX8hhFOsIrtRVIktOhASIX2TjAz93rEp4_p0-0Ol0cmMlnlF183WUnjxSMXRV0Mxyw6GxHRqKle02PeQpkCCm7vaGXKl-qwZs_hCD0QRscgvMQ8S_GplmT-c6LvUlvagZ89C42UJw8iuhpHNu8IzFoGMdudzKlWktxLJWDiWRJJUqPfBR1rnFpDcsUY25i1JVwAvoTUDmXVejvuBvtR3S7MZz9jgEkKHO4kME8IKGaJpJky14guxNr3Nt6YIGnYz7wzCzq8vWxMURMzFq_F-3bFYvQVB6toKe-hDQmqPx48kBsbu6qoh6hfH-aBRJJKh5UuqarYKZ7vNiU8dj9svit3dvsUK49KXdULFTqB5XXXzEvT39Pod20r4fnZia75bukqpix4u0dmBh7)

Meet Royce White:

**Born and raised in the Twin Cities, Royce White was the son of a single mother and started playing sports at the age of 5 years old. Living in various neighborhoods growing up, much of his family remained in the Rondo community, where he spent much of his time and received schooling. White excelled at basketball and recieved a scholarship to play basketball at the University of Minnesota, and later transferred to Iowa State. He would go on to make Dean's List while receiving All Big-12 first-team honors and many other basketball awards.

_After being selected 16th overall by The Houston Rockets in the NBA draft, White began to voice concerns about the NBA’s collective bargaining agreement (CBA), specifically regarding mental health policy, which at the time did not exist. Eventually, his concerns became a public dispute between White and the NBA. This resulted in White demanding that the NBA, as a representative of the global corporate community, take up the responsibility of spearheading the mental health conversation – not just posturing through press statements, but through policy._

Royce then went on to win two championships in Canada before moving over to play Big 3 Basketball. White appeared at a game in a t-shirt that read “Free The Uyghurs.” After a game-winning basket, White did a post-game interview on CBS, where he called for 2 Million Uyghurs to be freed and released from concentration camps. As the season went on, White would touch on taboo topics such as Gain of Function Research, The Federal Reserve, Julian Assange, Edward Snowden, Larry Hoover, Jeff Fort, Muammar Gaddafi, The Great Reset, Ivermectin, Me Too, etc._

Now, Royce White is running for Senate in Minnesota to take down the Radical Left. Republicans have not seen recent success in Minnesota, but Royce's campaign is different: a grassroots movement that's sick of the Radical Agenda pushed by Far Left Democrats Tina Smith and Ilhan Omar. Royce needs your help to pull off a fourth quarter comeback victory and FLIP Minnesota Red for Republicans.

** [**PASS ROYCE AN ASSIST**](https://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=ce1ce9e616484205a28a45cfaf96cecf&_m=63ce427bd4574794970b355b55b803eb&_e=MDAukdUJhUur8S50iwCDPeVuy7XYg-KR5P-pKTrRAd714tez0aIjitkMEd7w7GpwfQP6TCbp4TwR8749B66A_lUjsbOFgj9k8za75cpm2DL1bsgu0FBEh14YHFoSpnTMAJtfeiWLLnknw8Vu4bHeU6PEnnsUtGlbUUbfMtI7WwgPkym9DeBX8hhFOsIrtRVIqwyKIkjcY7pD-HITejK87S6KXxGUjOl3UzKdCgB7hOagVlyjyYiMG_zGMuonH9ZKf2TT_9Kkf_puMEz9V9ETufJoC-iMgtRRI-vZmp0BZG3E8snpumcV6KkhaQMvtbMlWKu53s5KanUREOLGpMkYRJDjJKNu0NtKXVMdAY4qLFB7C7Fq6udyR2PYKjzoLwVfkaWLE048laz7AI_Q-Tqz8J1LzRDg0jgblAXPFXROl1WioMvSfs4pOc6mBTcdGZV7Ntxz46z7ZInmXHBBaSs3pt7yEsEkWAcfZyACFRBsM0C3orgpdg5pNPKhMKC-HPqk9m8VwcAq_OfmM53WvSPJjLJS9wH_PuptQk4fdh8-ylqlKySO5--4eTMw6s9g15v4)

clean_body: We don't need Mykonos to love life | Peter, An American Airlines plane's landing gear caught fire on the Denver runway. Passengers had to evacuate down slides while smoke poured out the side of the jet. And they call me crazy for not flying. I’ve said it before, and I’ll repeat it: “I don’t like the sea. I don’t like the skies. I don’t like deep caves. I don’t want to go to space.” I’m not paranoid. I’m grounded. Literally. This isn’t about fear, it’s about freedom of thought. We’ve been trained to ignore our instincts, trust institutions that don’t earn it, and hand over control for the sake of “convenience.” But I’d rather take the long road and see America for what it is, not 35,000 feet above it, and not through a screen fed by the corporate state. We used to value simplicity, be grateful, and enjoy life without needing to post it from Mykonos or Mars. If you’re tired of the lies, the fear-mongering, the phony progress, then stand with me. | Let’s bring back common sense. Let’s bring back normal. Royce White Meet Royce White: Born and raised in the Twin Cities, Royce White was the son of a single mother and started playing sports at the age of 5 years old. Living in various neighborhoods growing up, much of his family remained in the Rondo community, where he spent much of his time and received schooling. White excelled at basketball and recieved a scholarship to play basketball at the University of Minnesota, and later transferred to Iowa State. He would go on to make Dean's List while receiving All Big-12 first-team honors and many other basketball awards. After being selected 16th overall by The Houston Rockets in the NBA draft, White began to voice concerns about the NBA’s collective bargaining agreement (CBA), specifically regarding mental health policy, which at the time did not exist. Eventually, his concerns became a public dispute between White and the NBA. This resulted in White demanding that the NBA, as a representative of the global corporate community, take up the responsibility of spearheading the mental health conversation – not just posturing through press statements, but through policy. Royce then went on to win two championships in Canada before moving over to play Big 3 Basketball. White appeared at a game in a t-shirt that read “Free The Uyghurs.” After a game-winning basket, White did a post-game interview on CBS, where he called for 2 Million Uyghurs to be freed and released from concentration camps. As the season went on, White would touch on taboo topics such as Gain of Function Research, The Federal Reserve, Julian Assange, Edward Snowden, Larry Hoover, Jeff Fort, Muammar Gaddafi, The Great Reset, Ivermectin, Me Too, etc. Now, Royce White is running for Senate in Minnesota to take down the Radical Left. Republicans have not seen recent success in Minnesota, but Royce's campaign is different: a grassroots movement that's sick of the Radical Agenda pushed by Far Left Democrats Tina Smith and Ilhan Omar. Royce needs your help to pull off a fourth quarter comeback victory and FLIP Minnesota Red for Republicans. PAID FOR BY ROYCE WHITE FOR SENATE P.O. Box 5473 Hopkins, MN 55343 Copyright (C) 2025 Royce White For Senate, All rights reserved.

name: Travis Tazelaar, VoteVets
subject: $200 mil on a gold ballroom while Vets lose their health care
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
Trump can afford to blow $200 million on a golden ballroom at the White House but he can’t afford to help Veterans, the elderly, sick and hungry kids.
#
Peter, Travis here.
You ever see something that instantly gets under your skin?
I’ll admit, it’s happening to me a lot more than usual this year. And yesterday, something happened that really, really set me off.
Trump announced he’s putting in a $200 million gilded ballroom at the White House. Yeah, they’re blowing $200 million on a big room for people to come and bow before Trump.
[](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-august25.hEpWPc&refcode=em250801-1-120&t=1&akid=34497%2E3013755%2ErHo04h)
That’s after Trump already paved over Jackie Kennedy’s Rose Garden. After he extorted Qatar for a $400 million jet, which is gonna cost $1 billion to get up to code, that he’s going to take personal possession of after he leaves office. After he threw himself a birthday parade at the cost of $45 million at _minimum._ After he’s taken something like 100 trips to his own golf courses on taxpayer dime — way more already than at this point in his first term.
Napkin math, that’s probably pushing $2 billion right there. And we’re not even getting into his crypto schemes and the global “deals” funnelling billions of dollars into his bank account.
You already know what’s happening back in the real world. The VA’s lost thousands of doctors, nurses, researchers, therapists, and caregivers. He’s selling off public lands to the highest bidder. He’s kicking literally hundreds of millions of people off their health care when they’re already struggling to get by. People can’t afford basic necessities because of his tariffs.
Trump can afford to blow $200 million on a golden ballroom at the White House but he can’t afford to help Veterans, the elderly, sick and hungry kids.
This is Marie Antoinette on steroids.
But that’s why I’m proud I get to work at VoteVets. It’s my job to find and build networks of support for thousands of Veterans around the country who decided to take a really tough step into public service. The only way we get to the other side of this is if people like them continue stepping up to put the country before themselves.
It’s not easy. It takes tons of work, and time away from your family and friends. You don’t get days off. It shouldn’t be this tough, but that’s the world we live in right now.
I’m proud of all of them. And I’m just so grateful I get to help them out.
The reality though, is that this work isn’t cheap. And sometimes it requires us making tough asks of the people who support us. Today is one of those days.
[**So, I’m humbly asking you: Can you make a donation to VoteVets today and help us lay the groundwork for taking back the House and the Senate next year? It’s our only shot at stopping Trump, and we’ve got to start now if we’re gonna have any chance.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-august25.hEpWPc&refcode=em250801-1-120&t=2&akid=34497%2E3013755%2ErHo04h)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_
| | **[Rush $10 now](https://act.votevets.org/go/2934?c0=vv-pac-em-august25.s0DXWu&refcode=em250801-1-120&t=21&akid=34497%2E3013755%2ErHo04h)**
---
****
| **[Rush $25 now](https://act.votevets.org/go/2935?c0=vv-pac-em-august25.c9qBG4&refcode=em250801-1-120&t=22&akid=34497%2E3013755%2ErHo04h)**
---
****

****

name: Tony Thurmond
subject: Kamala makes an announcement | California still under attack
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tony Thurmond for Governor 2026, FPPC# 1461509
body:
https://www.tonythurmond.com/ [https://www.tonythurmond.com/]

Friends,

As you likely have heard, Vice President Kamala Harris has announced that she will not be running for Governor. She has been a longtime friend, mentor and supporter, and I am deeply grateful for her incredible public service.

But as we celebrate her service and look forward to the next chapter, the Trump Administration’s unrelenting attacks on California aren’t stopping.

This week, I helped secure $1 billion in Federal education funding for California schools that had been illegally impounded by the Trump administration. And I’m doing everything in my power to protect immigrant students and families, sponsoring AB 49 to keep ICE off our campuses and supporting legal action to combat the Trump Administration’s egregious immigration abuses.

To help us continue to fight back against the Trump administration, DONATE TODAY [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/thurmondforgovernor] .

As your Governor, I will continue to defend Californians from the Trump Administration while building affordable housing, reducing homelessness, and helping working Californians secure good-paying jobs and reduce the cost of living so our economy works for everyday Californians.

Thanks for being on this journey to make a better California where working families can afford rent, food, and groceries.
For more information, visit tonythurmond.com [http://tonythurmond.com] or feel free to reach out to me at tony@tonythurmond.com if you wish to join us at some of our upcoming events.

In Solidarity,

Tony

clean_body: look who’s back from D.C... | = This week, Jon Husted will return to Ohio from Washington, D.C.. But let’s be clear: he didn’t come back to spend his recess speaking with his constituents, like most good leaders do... because he’s the reason Ohio families are facing skyrocketing healthcare costs, increased energy bills, job loss, and cuts to our kids’ education. Husted has been off in Washington, D.C., cozying up to Trump’s inner circle, Republican megadonors, and the dark money crowd he’s always answered to. Husted might be back in Ohio, but he’s never shown up for Ohio – he thinks if he hides from Ohioans, he can’t be held accountable for the damage he’s doing to our nation. Ohio deserves a Senator who fights for our working families, not billionaires, not super PACs, and definitely not Trump’s personal agenda. = _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately: = = = = = = Thank you, Ohio Dems = | Ohio Democratic Party PO Box 130 Columbus, Ohio 43216 | Paid for by the Ohio Democratic Party and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. | Click here to from the Ohio Democratic Party's email list.

name: EMILYs List Campaigns
subject: You in for August, Peter?
body:
Political insiders often call August "slow," but with so much on the line for our party and country, we know better. |
---
[](https://click.everyaction.com/k/112582004/560952919/-140265690?refcode=WUEFE801_20250801_AugustToplines_ALL_v1s3&sourceid=1056716&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9UU00vVFNNRUwvMS84NzA2OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOTZhZmY5Yy0xMDZmLWYwMTEtOGRjOS02MDQ1YmRhOWQ5NmIiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pPlnXpk9fT7nfCUnNQzKMeKDwYXnnbUrJeFjiALpvFw=) Peter,

August might be called an "off" month in politics, but with everything happening across the country, **we know it's anything but quiet. ** Not when extremist Republicans continue to build momentum.
Not when early voting is happening RIGHT NOW in key primaries.
Not when so many of our candidates need support to stay competitive.

[**So no, we're not easing into August. We're doubling down, and we need you with us. name: David Shadburn, LCV
subject: TAKE ACTION: Tell the Trump administration not to weaken EPA standards for power plant pollution
body:
If you are unable to see the message below, [click here to view](https://links.messages.lcv.org/servlet/MailView?ms=MTI3Nzc4MjgS1&r=NjA3MDU0MTAzNTc3S0&j=MjI4MDAyNjkzMQS2&mt=1&rt=0).
| | | |
---
| |
## **TAKE ACTION: Tell the Trump administration not to weaken EPA**

Trump and EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin are proposing to repeal all greenhouse gas emissions standards for fossil fuel-fired power plants.
There is a comment period open now, when we can make our voices heard. [**_Tell Lee Zeldin and the EPA NOT to finalize this dangerous rule that will pollute the air we breathe, worsen climate change, and endanger public health._**](https://links.messages.lcv.)
---
| | | [ TAKE ACTION ](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/RZbkcRLxm~)
---
| | | | Trump and EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin are proposing to repeal all greenhouse gas emissions standards for fossil fuel-fired power plants. This rollback of Clean Air Act emissions standards would be **extremely dangerous** for both public health and the climate. The air will be dirtier, people will be sicker, and the climate crisis will continue to get worse. There is a comment period open now where we can make our voices heard. [**_Tell Lee Zeldin and the EPA NOT to finalize this dangerous rule that will pollute the air we breathe, worsen climate change, and endanger public health._**](https://links.messages.lcv.)
---
| | | [ TAKE ACTION ](https://links.messages.lcv.org/els/v2/w_)
---
| | Carbon emissions from power plants are the second-largest source of climate pollution in the U.S., and it is contributing significantly to climate change. A hotter climate means more severe weather events like droughts, storms, and floods, more heat-related illnesses and deaths, and higher energy bills for consumers, just to name a few impacts. Our country should be doing significantly more to fight the climate crisis, not turning our back on climate action. We also know that weakening critical safeguards and pollution reduction measures would be harmful to public health, leading to increases in asthma and respiratory illnesses, especially for communities living near these power plants.
---
| | The EPA should be prioritizing the health of our families and communities, rather than the profits of polluters. The deadline for public comments is coming soon. [**_Tell the EPA NOT to finalize this dangerous rule that weakens the Clean Air Act, worsens climate change and endangers public health. _**](https://links.messages.lcv.)
---
| | Thanks for taking action, Peter.
---
| | David Shadburn
Legislative Director
League of Conservation Voters
---

name: Heidi Campbell
subject: Public education is on the line in Tennessee
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Campbell for State Senate, Lee Jones, treasurer
body:
https://voteheidicampbell.com/ [https://voteheidicampbell.com/]
Peter,
As the new school year begins, Tennessee’s public education system is under threat — not from lack of talent or dedication, but from political agendas that put ideology over what’s best for our kids.
Republican lawmakers in the state legislature remain determined to divert public funds to private schools. These schemes don’t create opportunity. They weaken the public schools that serve most Tennessee children.
As your state senator, I’ve stood with parents, teachers, and communities to resist this reckless agenda, and I’ll keep working to ensure every child, in every zip code, has access to a safe, well-funded public school.
I hope you’ll consider chipping in today so we can ensure our campaign remains strong and I have the resources to continue advocating for every Tennessee child in the legislature. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/heidiemail2025?refcode=0801backtoschool]
I hope every Tennessee family has a great start to the school year, and I look forward to new opportunities to stand up for our children, educators, and communities in Tennessee. Please don’t hesitate to reach out to me if I can be helpful in any way.
Heidi
DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/heidiemail2025?refcode=0801backtoschool]
Senator Heidi Campbell is a Tennessee State Senator, representing District 20. She is championing change for Tennessee and won't rest until we see gun safety reform, corporate accountability, and a safe and prosperous community where everyone can thrive.

name: Richard Blumenthal
subject: What matters most to you right now
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Blumenthal for Connecticut
body:
| | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112582938/560956139/623074613?refcode=jtk1411-su-nd1&code=jtk1411-su-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZGZlODJkY2QtMTI2Zi1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=KEjgWxTanLaV6pomavVDgTpFcUH44lb6ZD0D9fCHHqk=&emci=3c40d1c1-f26e-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=dfe82dcd-126f-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=9329426)
---
Friend,

During a chapter in our nation's history that is filled with so much uncertainty and chaos, you have my word that I am fighting each and every day in the Senate for you and your family. **That's why it's so important for me to hear about your biggest priorities and concerns.**
As Donald Trump and this GOP-led Senate threaten everything from healthcare access to our veterans' hard-earned benefits, to our national security, to so much more, I know that there is no shortage of issues weighing heavy on your mind. My team and I have put together a brand-new survey for you to share your thoughts with me directly.
**I'm looking for 1,000 members of this team to complete my 2025 Priorities Survey before midnight tonight, and I sincerely hope you'll take a moment to share your responses. **

**[Please, will you take this survey and let me know what's top of mind for you and your family right now? It will only take a minute or two, and your input will be crucial in shaping my priorities in the Senate.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112582939/560956141/1407439886?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&ask=5&refcode=jtk1411-su-nd1&code=jtk1411-su-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZGZlODJkY2QtMTI2Zi1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=KEjgWxTanLaV6pomavVDgTpFcUH44lb6ZD0D9fCHHqk=&emci=3c40d1c1-f26e-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=dfe82dcd-126f-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=9329426)**
| | [Take the Survey](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/112582940/560956142/1407439886?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&amounts=5,25,50,100,250&ask=5&refcode=jtk1411-su-nd1&code=jtk1411-su-nd1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDM3OTQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiZGZlODJkY2QtMTI2Zi1mMDExLThkYzktNjA0NWJkYTlkOTZiIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=KEjgWxTanLaV6pomavVDgTpFcUH44lb6ZD0D9fCHHqk=&emci=3c40d1c1-f26e-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&emdi=dfe82dcd-126f-f011-8dc9-6045bda9d96b&ceid=9329426)
---
I've always believed that listening is one of the most important things a public servant can do. Thank you for helping me become a better advocate for you and your family.

Sincerely,

Dick

* **TOP NEWS**

**" Ghislaine Maxwell transferred to prison camp in Texas," **via AP**** -- "**Jeffrey Epstein 's** former girlfriend, **Ghislaine Maxwell** , has been moved from a federal prison in Florida to a prison camp in Texas as her criminal case generates renewed public attention.

The federal Bureau of Prisons said Friday that Maxwell had been transferred to Bryan but did not explain the circumstances. Her attorney confirmed the move but also declined to discuss the reasons for it.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $7/mo](https://substack.com/redirect/91578a9c-b94a-4fdd-bf5f-08545396d8aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
Maxwell was convicted in 2021 of helping Epstein sexually abuse underage girls and was [sentenced to 20 years in prison](https://substack.com/redirect/6560b2ff-0b2b-449b-b1da-cd7fe78e25d4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). She had been held at a prison in Tallahassee, Fla., until her transfer to the camp in Texas, where other inmates include [Theranos founder](https://substack.com/redirect/7e680e55-1f33-4da1-9543-2011e9808fd6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) **Elizabeth Holmes** and **Jen Shah** of _The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City_.

Minimum-security federal prison camps house inmates the Bureau of Prisons considers to be the lowest security risk. Some don't even have fences.
[BECOME A PAID SUBSCRIBER: $70/yr](https://substack.com/redirect/91578a9c-b94a-4fdd-bf5f-08545396d8aa?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
The prison camps were originally designed with low security to make operations easier and to allow inmates tasked with performing work at the prison, like landscaping and maintenance, to avoid repeatedly checking in and out of a main prison facility.

Maxwell's case has been the subject of heightened public focus since an outcry over the Justice Department's statement last month saying that it would not be releasing any additional documents from the Epstein sex trafficking investigation.

Since then, administration officials have suggested they are promoting transparency in the case, including by requesting the unsealing of grand jury transcripts.

Maxwell was interviewed at a Florida courthouse over two days last week by Deputy Attorney General **Todd Blanche**.

The [House Oversight Committee](https://substack.com/redirect/668734fa-4dd4-45a2-aef4-8b8eaa27a8fe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) has separately said that it wants to speak with Maxwell as well. Her lawyers said this week that she would be open to an interview but only if the panel were to give her immunity from prosecution for anything she said." [AP](https://substack.com/redirect/c8422368-9927-44cc-a6c8-8d9d98aa1d1d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)

* **#TXLEGE**

**" Takeaways from the heated July 4 flood hearing: Kerr County officials grilled over absences, delays," **Houston Chronicle's**Amanda Drane** ,**Neena Satija** and**Megan Kimble** \-- "Flood relief is one of many items the Texas Legislature is taking up in the 30-day special session called by Gov. **Greg Abbott**. Lawmakers have yet to put forward a comprehensive agenda, but Thursday's testimony could help shape legislation that is eventually filed.

Here are the key takeaways so far:

**Dan Patrick called out Kerr County Judge Rob Kelly**

Lt. Gov. **Dan Patrick** said he was in Kerr County on July 4 and had a series of meetings with county officials, yet never saw County Judge **Rob Kelly** that day.

"This is about as bad a day as you can have, and I've had a lot of bad days. We didn't just lose people; we didn't know where people were," Patrick said. "And you weren't here."

Kelly had earlier testified he was staying at his lake home in Travis County overnight as the flooding began, but headed back to Kerr County after he woke up on July 4. At one point that day, Kelly said, he went home to check on his wife.

Patrick told the judge he should have been at the county's emergency center.

"I don't know where you were on Day One, on July 4, but you should have been here. You should have been here directing that response. That is your responsibility ... Everyone was here that day working their ass off and you were nowhere to be found."

**Local officials were asleep, out sick when flooding started**

The three top emergency officials in Kerr County all testified they were not working until after the floods began.

County Judge Kelly testified he woke up to phone calls and messages from Texas Division of Emergency Management **Nim Kidd** , Kerr County Sheriff **Larry Leitha** and Kerrville City Manager **Dalton Rice** in the "5, 5:30 range" on the morning of July 4.

By then, he said, flooding [had already overtaken Camp Mystic](https://substack.com/redirect/928b9d78-1a96-4ba5-979d-b40e0a3786da?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and other camps.

He said he received no "no alerts suggesting an extreme weather event was imminent," even though he gets a lot of alerts about weather.

"Something I could never have imagined," he said, adding the National Weather Service didn't forecast this "in a timely manner."

Leitha said he woke up at 4:20 a.m.: "I started calling people out. A little later, I did talk to the judge (Kelly)."

**W.B. "Dub" Thomas**, the county's emergency management coordinator, said he was ill and on paid time off on July 3 in the run-up to the deadly storm. He said he was awoken at 5:30 a.m. on July 4 by a caller asking him to respond to the floods.

"Based on the data we had at the time, there was no indication that a catastrophic flood was imminent," Thomas said.

The three top emergency management officials for Kerr County -- the county judge, the sheriff, and emergency management coordinator -- pointed out numerous times that some emergency responders were awake and responding to the flooding as early as 3 a.m. But lawmakers from both parties pushed back on that defense.

"The three guys in Kerr County who were responsible for sounding the alarm were effectively unavailable. Am I hearing that right?" asked state Rep. **Ann Johnson** , D-Houston.

"Knowing now what you know, is there a protocol that needs to be put in place? That, if the three folks who are responsible at this moment are not available for whatever reason, what should we do?" Johnson asked.

"Yes ma'am, we can look at it real hard," Leitha responded. "Maybe they can call me earlier."

State Rep. **Drew Darby** , R-San Angelo, echoed Johnson's concerns and said he found the county officials' answers "very disturbing."

"We have people dying," he said, "and yeah, we have people charged with responsibility for their protection -- but they're in their beds, they're sick, they're waiting for somebody else."

Johnson also asked Leitha and Thomas if they were aware that as early as 2 a.m., according to reports she said she'd heard, "there were little girls with water around their feet" at Camp Mystic on the Guadalupe River.

They said no.

"I've had discussions with the camps before, and the process was, they need to notify the sheriff's office," Thomas said. Leitha added that he had no indication his office had received any such notifications at that time.

**Kerr County criticized over delayed alerts, lack of evacuation notice**

Under Texas law, Kelly, the county judge, would have been responsible for deciding whether or not to issue evacuation orders for the county. Asked why he did not, he told lawmakers that it was "too late" and would have resulted in "one heck of a traffic jam" just as waters were overtaking roadways.

State Sen. **Paul Bettencourt** , R-Houston, said he understood that evacuations by road could have been too dangerous, but believed something still could have been done as weather forecasters were issuing flood warnings, particularly after an emergency alert went out not long after 4 a.m.

"Would there not have been a recognition of the obvious that there needs to be an alert that says, effectively, get to higher ground?" Bettencourt said.

"I agree," Kelly responded. Left unaddressed in the exchange was the fact that Kelly had apparently been asleep at that point and so would not have been able to make such a call.

Officials also acknowledged that a "CodeRed" alert regarding the flooding, which people in Kerr County could have received on their phones as long as they hadn't opted out, wasn't sent until 5:01 a.m - more than 40 minutes after a dispatcher had requested that it be sent.

**Timeline of flood response emerges**

For the first time, local officials with the City of Kerrville and Kerr County publicly provided a more detailed account of when emergency dispatchers began receiving urgent calls in the early morning hours of July 4.

At 3:34 a.m. -- more than two hours after the National Weather Service had issued a flood alert for portions of Kerr County -- emergency dispatchers received the first phone call from a family that was "on a roof requesting air evacuation" that led county officials to realize they had an "all hands on deck" situation, Leitha said.

After that, call volume increased quickly, according to timelines provided by local officials. In the next hour, dispatchers began hearing from children trapped in flooding homes. Because of rising waters, some sheriff's deputies became stranded, too. Calls from Camp Mystic came in at 3:57 a.m., Leitha said, reporting people were "stranded on a hill seeking evacuation."

While some Kerr County sheriff's deputies and other area first responders were awake at that point and saved many lives, Leitha said, it appeared that they were doing so while he and the county's emergency management official -- emergency coordinator Thomas -- were not yet awake. Still, Leitha reiterated that even weather forecasters had not foreseen the level of danger.

As an example, he noted that at 4:32 a.m. -- a few minutes after a sheriff's deputy reported hearing "kids screaming in the river" -- the National Weather Service called the sheriff's office to ask if roads were closed and if flooding was occurring.

Leitha paused and shook his head after delivering that testimony to lawmakers.

**Flood itself 'worth studying'**

Kerr County commissioned an independent hydrology assessment that confirms that "this was a 1,000-year flood," Kerr County Judge Kelly said. He described the event as "very much worth studying." It also sat "in a location with very few rain monitors or flood gauges."

The storm was "not typical for our weather patterns" because it stayed, "it did not pass through, it overwhelmed the watershed," he said.

"This was not foreseeable," Kelly said. It "reveals the urgent need for stronger tools, better detection, and modernized infrastructure."

**Lawmakers again grill leaders of the Upper Guadalupe River Authority**

Last week, lawmakers blasted the general manager of the Upper Guadalupe River Authority -- which covers the Guadalupe River in Kerr County alone -- for [choosing to cut property taxes rather than modernize](https://substack.com/redirect/319ed5a5-ad3c-4193-aeaf-ed0b8e30c84d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) its flood warning system, citing an investigation by the Houston Chronicle.

On Thursday, Darby, a Republican from San Angelo, again grilled UGRA board chair **William Rector** over that decision. "It was recognized through studies that you paid for that there was a need for an early warning system, in your words, an alert system would have saved lives. And yet you didn't do anything about it," Darby said.

Rector, who was appointed by Abbott said that the river authority had grown so frustrated with its efforts to secure funding for flood warning that "we said we're going to do it ourselves. We're going to just take the bull by the horn. We're going to create our own system," he said.

Rector said that in January, UGRA issued a request for proposals to install such a system. "By June 4, we had actually hired a contractor, signed a contract to begin creating a system with our own money that would support flood warning."

But the UGRA has so far approved little more than a "software system" consisting of a public-facing dashboard, rather than additional river gauges to collect more data, Rector confirmed during the hearing. Earlier this year the board approved spending less than $73,000 on the project, [documents show](https://substack.com/redirect/3c7bb2df-2f99-491c-814d-af1350ff67c3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).

Rector told lawmakers Thursday that the river authority more recently committed to spending $1.5 million from its reserves to conduct further studies on the issue.

State Rep. **Ken King** , a Republican and the chairman of the House committee, noted that UGRA's board is appointed by the governor.

"So you're not accountable to the voters of Kerr County?" he asked. "They couldn't not elect you and take you off the board, if they didn't think you were doing a good job?"

"That's correct," Rector said.

State Sen. **Donna Campbell** , a Republican from New Braunfels, suggested that the Upper Guadalupe River Authority might be absorbed by the larger Guadalupe Blanco River Authority, which covers 10 counties downstream.

"Guadalupe Blanco has water production and sewer services they can sell so if we were merged you would be asking people downstream to support us in Kerr County," Rector said.

"It's a river authority that is managed for one county only and I'm not sure there weren't things that could have been done over the years ... that possibly could have helped," Campbell said.

**Residents plead for more help with recovery**

Residents told harrowing stories of escaping rapidly rising July 4 flood waters and asked lawmakers to spend more on recovery efforts -- still ongoing weeks later -- and invest in warning systems, evacuation routes and more to save lives in future storms.

**Ann Carr** of Ingram told the panel she lives on Ingram Lake, which is still so full of debris it has become a "toxic pit." She said she has spoken to divers who have been in the lake and told her there are still bodies in the water. And she said an oil slick "continues to bubble up right in front of our property" from a submerged vehicle.

"We have six feet of rubble that has invaded our lake, and it's become a graveyard," Carr said.

She urged the state to dip into its massive rainy day fund to drain the lake and clean it out, arguing it is urgent for the survival of the community.

"That lake in Ingram is the heart of our city," Carr said. "It brings money into our city. We have people that come in and boat, fish. They spend money at the restaurants. We're a poor community, and we need that lake drained."

**Nancy Zdunkewicz** told the panel her family awoke at 3 a.m. and the flood water was already at their house in Hunt, about 40 feet above the normal water level.

Her family tried to get to higher ground, but was instead forced to climb into trees on their property and wait out the flood, watching as cars and other trees were washed away.

She called for more evacuation routes and warning systems.

"This community really needs help with recovery, and we need preparation for the next flood," Zdunkewicz said. "Had there been early detection of the rising waters and sirens, we may have been able to leave in time to get to higher ground safely."

**County officials request more resources**

Kelly said his county would need a flood warning and detection system to better predict future floods, more interconnectivity to address cell phone dead zones, and more resources for emergency management to better address future floods.

Much of the western portion of the county has very poor cell service, he said.

"Even if people wanted to call .. and warn folks ... they didn't have the ability," he said.

Rice said there needs to be investments in localized predictive weather modeling and expanded broadband infrastructure.

"Kerrville will rebuild, but we need the tools and the funding to do it right," he said.

**Texas ' top GOP leaders are in attendance**

Lt. Gov. Patrick and House Speaker **Dustin Burrows** don't serve on legislative committees and usually don't attend public hearings, but they showed up to this one in Kerrville.

In opening statements, Patrick, a Republican who presides over the state Senate, said this hearing is just the beginning.

"We will pass bills dealing with the immediacy of things that we can address now, but we will be doing this in the next session and the session after that," he told the crowd. "We will not quit until you feel like we've done everything that we can do that you've asked us to do."

Burrows, R-Lubbock, encouraged officials to be forthright in their testimony.

"We cannot come up with solutions, we cannot try to improve things, if we don't know everything that you can share with us," he said." [Hou Chron](https://substack.com/redirect/b16cd4a8-dddb-49d8-a283-9130d1fa68ee?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) ($)

TOP NEWS " Ghislaine Maxwell transferred to prison camp in Texas," via AP** -- "Jeffrey Epstein 's former girlfriend, Ghislaine Maxwell , has been moved from a federal prison in Florida to a prison camp in Texas as her criminal case generates renewed public attention. The federal Bureau of Prisons said Friday that Maxwell had been transferred to Bryan but did not explain the circumstances. Her attorney confirmed the move but also declined to discuss the reasons for it. Maxwell was convicted in 2021 of helping Epstein sexually abuse underage girls and was . She had been held at a prison in Tallahassee, Fla., until her transfer to the camp in Texas, where other inmates include Elizabeth Holmes and Jen Shah of The Real Housewives of Salt Lake City. Minimum-security federal prison camps house inmates the Bureau of Prisons considers to be the lowest security risk. Some don't even have fences. The prison camps were originally designed with low security to make operations easier and to allow inmates tasked with performing work at the prison, like landscaping and maintenance, to avoid repeatedly checking in and out of a main prison facility. Maxwell's case has been the subject of heightened public focus since an outcry over the Justice Department's statement last month saying that it would not be releasing any additional documents from the Epstein sex trafficking investigation. Since then, administration officials have suggested they are promoting transparency in the case, including by requesting the unsealing of grand jury transcripts. Maxwell was interviewed at a Florida courthouse over two days last week by Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche. The has separately said that it wants to speak with Maxwell as well. Her lawyers said this week that she would be open to an interview but only if the panel were to give her immunity from prosecution for anything she said." #TXLEGE " Takeaways from the heated July 4 flood hearing: Kerr County officials grilled over absences, delays," Houston Chronicle'sAmanda Drane ,Neena Satija andMegan Kimble \-- "Flood relief is one of many items the Texas Legislature is taking up in the 30-day special session called by Gov. Greg Abbott. Lawmakers have yet to put forward a comprehensive agenda, but Thursday's testimony could help shape legislation that is eventually filed. Here are the key takeaways so far: Dan Patrick called out Kerr County Judge Rob Kelly Lt. Gov. Dan Patrick said he was in Kerr County on July 4 and had a series of meetings with county officials, yet never saw County Judge Rob Kelly that day. "This is about as bad a day as you can have, and I've had a lot of bad days. We didn't just lose people; we didn't know where people were," Patrick said. "And you weren't here." Kelly had earlier testified he was staying at his lake home in Travis County overnight as the flooding began, but headed back to Kerr County after he woke up on July 4. At one point that day, Kelly said, he went home to check on his wife. Patrick told the judge he should have been at the county's emergency center. "I don't know where you were on Day One, on July 4, but you should have been here. You should have been here directing that response. That is your responsibility ... Everyone was here that day working their ass off and you were nowhere to be found." Local officials were asleep, out sick when flooding started The three top emergency officials in Kerr County all testified they were not working until after the floods began. County Judge Kelly testified he woke up to phone calls and messages from Texas Division of Emergency Management Nim Kidd , Kerr County Sheriff Larry Leitha and Kerrville City Manager Dalton Rice in the "5, 5:30 range" on the morning of July 4. By then, he said, flooding and other camps. He said he received no "no alerts suggesting an extreme weather event was imminent," even though he gets a lot of alerts about weather. "Something I could never have imagined," he said, adding the National Weather Service didn't forecast this "in a timely manner." Leitha said he woke up at 4:20 a.m.: "I started calling people out. A little later, I did talk to the judge (Kelly)." W.B. "Dub" Thomas, the county's emergency management coordinator, said he was ill and on paid time off on July 3 in the run-up to the deadly storm. He said he was awoken at 5:30 a.m. on July 4 by a caller asking him to respond to the floods. "Based on the data we had at the time, there was no indication that a catastrophic flood was imminent," Thomas said. The three top emergency management officials for Kerr County -- the county judge, the sheriff, and emergency management coordinator -- pointed out numerous times that some emergency responders were awake and responding to the flooding as early as 3 a.m. But lawmakers from both parties pushed back on that defense. "The three guys in Kerr County who were responsible for sounding the alarm were effectively unavailable. Am I hearing that right?" asked state Rep. Ann Johnson , D-Houston. "Knowing now what you know, is there a protocol that needs to be put in place? That, if the three folks who are responsible at this moment are not available for whatever reason, what should we do?" Johnson asked. "Yes ma'am, we can look at it real hard," Leitha responded. "Maybe they can call me earlier." State Rep. Drew Darby , R-San Angelo, echoed Johnson's concerns and said he found the county officials' answers "very disturbing." "We have people dying," he said, "and yeah, we have people charged with responsibility for their protection -- but they're in their beds, they're sick, they're waiting for somebody else." Johnson also asked Leitha and Thomas if they were aware that as early as 2 a.m., according to reports she said she'd heard, "there were little girls with water around their feet" at Camp Mystic on the Guadalupe River. They said no. "I've had discussions with the camps before, and the process was, they need to notify the sheriff's office," Thomas said. Leitha added that he had no indication his office had received any such notifications at that time. Kerr County criticized over delayed alerts, lack of evacuation notice Under Texas law, Kelly, the county judge, would have been responsible for deciding whether or not to issue evacuation orders for the county. Asked why he did not, he told lawmakers that it was "too late" and would have resulted in "one heck of a traffic jam" just as waters were overtaking roadways. State Sen. Paul Bettencourt , R-Houston, said he understood that evacuations by road could have been too dangerous, but believed something still could have been done as weather forecasters were issuing flood warnings, particularly after an emergency alert went out not long after 4 a.m. "Would there not have been a recognition of the obvious that there needs to be an alert that says, effectively, get to higher ground?" Bettencourt said. "I agree," Kelly responded. Left unaddressed in the exchange was the fact that Kelly had apparently been asleep at that point and so would not have been able to make such a call. Officials also acknowledged that a "CodeRed" alert regarding the flooding, which people in Kerr County could have received on their phones as long as they hadn't opted out, wasn't sent until 5:01 a.m - more than 40 minutes after a dispatcher had requested that it be sent. Timeline of flood response emerges For the first time, local officials with the City of Kerrville and Kerr County publicly provided a more detailed account of when emergency dispatchers began receiving urgent calls in the early morning hours of July 4. At 3:34 a.m. -- more than two hours after the National Weather Service had issued a flood alert for portions of Kerr County -- emergency dispatchers received the first phone call from a family that was "on a roof requesting air evacuation" that led county officials to realize they had an "all hands on deck" situation, Leitha said. After that, call volume increased quickly, according to timelines provided by local officials. In the next hour, dispatchers began hearing from children trapped in flooding homes. Because of rising waters, some sheriff's deputies became stranded, too. Calls from Camp Mystic came in at 3:57 a.m., Leitha said, reporting people were "stranded on a hill seeking evacuation." While some Kerr County sheriff's deputies and other area first responders were awake at that point and saved many lives, Leitha said, it appeared that they were doing so while he and the county's emergency management official -- emergency coordinator Thomas -- were not yet awake. Still, Leitha reiterated that even weather forecasters had not foreseen the level of danger. As an example, he noted that at 4:32 a.m. -- a few minutes after a sheriff's deputy reported hearing "kids screaming in the river" -- the National Weather Service called the sheriff's office to ask if roads were closed and if flooding was occurring. Leitha paused and shook his head after delivering that testimony to lawmakers. Flood itself 'worth studying' Kerr County commissioned an independent hydrology assessment that confirms that "this was a 1,000-year flood," Kerr County Judge Kelly said. He described the event as "very much worth studying." It also sat "in a location with very few rain monitors or flood gauges." The storm was "not typical for our weather patterns" because it stayed, "it did not pass through, it overwhelmed the watershed," he said. "This was not foreseeable," Kelly said. It "reveals the urgent need for stronger tools, better detection, and modernized infrastructure." Lawmakers again grill leaders of the Upper Guadalupe River Authority Last week, lawmakers blasted the general manager of the Upper Guadalupe River Authority -- which covers the Guadalupe River in Kerr County alone -- for its flood warning system, citing an investigation by the Houston Chronicle. On Thursday, Darby, a Republican from San Angelo, again grilled UGRA board chair William Rector over that decision. "It was recognized through studies that you paid for that there was a need for an early warning system, in your words, an alert system would have saved lives. And yet you didn't do anything about it," Darby said. Rector, who was appointed by Abbott said that the river authority had grown so frustrated with its efforts to secure funding for flood warning that "we said we're going to do it ourselves. We're going to just take the bull by the horn. We're going to create our own system," he said. Rector said that in January, UGRA issued a request for proposals to install such a system. "By June 4, we had actually hired a contractor, signed a contract to begin creating a system with our own money that would support flood warning." But the UGRA has so far approved little more than a "software system" consisting of a public-facing dashboard, rather than additional river gauges to collect more data, Rector confirmed during the hearing. Earlier this year the board approved spending less than $73,000 on the project, . Rector told lawmakers Thursday that the river authority more recently committed to spending $1.5 million from its reserves to conduct further studies on the issue. State Rep. Ken King , a Republican and the chairman of the House committee, noted that UGRA's board is appointed by the governor. "So you're not accountable to the voters of Kerr County?" he asked. "They couldn't not elect you and take you off the board, if they didn't think you were doing a good job?" "That's correct," Rector said. State Sen. Donna Campbell , a Republican from New Braunfels, suggested that the Upper Guadalupe River Authority might be absorbed by the larger Guadalupe Blanco River Authority, which covers 10 counties downstream. "Guadalupe Blanco has water production and sewer services they can sell so if we were merged you would be asking people downstream to support us in Kerr County," Rector said. "It's a river authority that is managed for one county only and I'm not sure there weren't things that could have been done over the years ... that possibly could have helped," Campbell said. Residents plead for more help with recovery Residents told harrowing stories of escaping rapidly rising July 4 flood waters and asked lawmakers to spend more on recovery efforts -- still ongoing weeks later -- and invest in warning systems, evacuation routes and more to save lives in future storms. Ann Carr of Ingram told the panel she lives on Ingram Lake, which is still so full of debris it has become a "toxic pit." She said she has spoken to divers who have been in the lake and told her there are still bodies in the water. And she said an oil slick "continues to bubble up right in front of our property" from a submerged vehicle. "We have six feet of rubble that has invaded our lake, and it's become a graveyard," Carr said. She urged the state to dip into its massive rainy day fund to drain the lake and clean it out, arguing it is urgent for the survival of the community. "That lake in Ingram is the heart of our city," Carr said. "It brings money into our city. We have people that come in and boat, fish. They spend money at the restaurants. We're a poor community, and we need that lake drained." Nancy Zdunkewicz told the panel her family awoke at 3 a.m. and the flood water was already at their house in Hunt, about 40 feet above the normal water level. Her family tried to get to higher ground, but was instead forced to climb into trees on their property and wait out the flood, watching as cars and other trees were washed away. She called for more evacuation routes and warning systems. "This community really needs help with recovery, and we need preparation for the next flood," Zdunkewicz said. "Had there been early detection of the rising waters and sirens, we may have been able to leave in time to get to higher ground safely." County officials request more resources Kelly said his county would need a flood warning and detection system to better predict future floods, more interconnectivity to address cell phone dead zones, and more resources for emergency management to better address future floods. Much of the western portion of the county has very poor cell service, he said. "Even if people wanted to call .. and warn folks ... they didn't have the ability," he said. Rice said there needs to be investments in localized predictive weather modeling and expanded broadband infrastructure. "Kerrville will rebuild, but we need the tools and the funding to do it right," he said. Texas ' top GOP leaders are in attendance Lt. Gov. Patrick and House Speaker Dustin Burrows don't serve on legislative committees and usually don't attend public hearings, but they showed up to this one in Kerrville. In opening statements, Patrick, a Republican who presides over the state Senate, said this hearing is just the beginning. "We will pass bills dealing with the immediacy of things that we can address now, but we will be doing this in the next session and the session after that," he told the crowd. "We will not quit until you feel like we've done everything that we can do that you've asked us to do." Burrows, R-Lubbock, encouraged officials to be forthright in their testimony. "We cannot come up with solutions, we cannot try to improve things, if we don't know everything that you can share with us," he said."
